name: monitoring-analytics
description: Monitor Proxmox infrastructure health and performance. Track node statistics, analyze resource utilization, and identify optimization opportunities across your cluster.
capabilities: Get node statistics and performance metrics from Proxmox cluster
Monitor CPU, memory, and disk utilization across all nodes
Track VM and container performance data with detailed metrics
Analyze overall cluster resource allocation and identify optimization opportunities
author: DataKnifeAI
securitySummary: This is a pure markdown documentation file with no executable code, scripts, or network capabilities. The skill defines prompt templates and tool descriptions for a Proxmox monitoring MCP integration. All 38 static findings are false positives caused by the scanner flagging markdown documentation as executable code.
valueStatement: Managing Proxmox infrastructure requires tracking multiple nodes, VMs, and containers. This skill provides unified access to cluster metrics, helping you identify bottlenecks and optimize resource allocation across your environment.
userTitle: Monitor Proxmox infrastructure health and performance
limitations: Requires Proxmox VE MCP server to be configured and running
Cannot modify resources, only read metrics and performance data
Historical data availability depends on MCP server configuration
Network performance monitoring requires additional storage tool configuration

useCases.title: Cluster Health Monitoring
useCases.description: Get comprehensive health status of all nodes and workloads in your Proxmox cluster

useCases.title: Performance Bottleneck Detection
useCases.description: Identify which nodes, VMs, or containers are causing performance issues

useCases.title: Capacity Planning
useCases.description: Analyze resource trends to plan infrastructure scaling and upgrades

promptTemplates.title: Quick Health Check
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me the current cluster health and performance status

promptTemplates.title: Resource Analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Which nodes are running at high CPU or memory utilization

promptTemplates.title: VM Performance
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me performance metrics for all virtual machines

promptTemplates.title: Full Analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate a complete performance analysis report for my Proxmox cluster including node status, VM metrics, and optimization recommendations

bestPractices: Establish baseline metrics before making infrastructure changes to measure impact accurately
Set alert thresholds for CPU, memory, and storage utilization to catch issues early
Review metrics regularly to identify gradual performance degradation before it becomes critical
antiPatterns: Waiting until resources are at 100% before investigating performance issues
Ignoring gradual increases in resource utilization over time until problems occur
Making infrastructure changes without comparing before and after metrics

faq.question: Which Proxmox versions are supported?
faq.answer: Works with Proxmox VE 7.x and 8.x through the Proxmox VE MCP server integration.

faq.question: How frequently are metrics updated?
faq.answer: Metric freshness depends on your MCP server configuration. Check your server settings for update intervals.

faq.question: Can I integrate this with external monitoring tools?
faq.answer: This skill reads from Proxmox MCP. Export metrics to Grafana, Prometheus, or other tools via their respective integrations.

faq.question: Is my Proxmox data sent anywhere?
faq.answer: No. The skill queries your local Proxmox MCP server. Data stays within your infrastructure.

faq.question: Why am I getting authentication errors?
faq.answer: Ensure your Proxmox MCP server is configured with valid credentials and can reach your Proxmox API endpoint.

faq.question: How does this compare to Proxmox built-in monitoring?
faq.answer: This skill provides AI-powered analysis and natural language summaries. Use both tools together for comprehensive coverage.
